mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations. This positive sentiment translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits. This strong demand environment drives S&P 500 forward returns positively as investors anticipate improved financial performance.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month annualized growth consistently exceeds 5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low and the federal funds rate is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital for businesses and increases the present value of future earnings, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the specified trigger conditions, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of such periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during an economic contraction signals deteriorating labor market conditions. This dampens consumer spending, reduces corporate profits, and erodes investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic outlook worsens.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months during a confirmed contraction, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a strong and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and improved investor confidence. This positive economic backdrop drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as future growth prospects are priced in.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 1-year growth consistently exceeds 2.5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of these periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This negatively impacts corporate revenues, profit margins, and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations are lowered.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 2.0% annualized over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts indicates strong construction activity and robust consumer confidence in the housing market. This has positive spillover effects on the broader economy and corporate earnings, supporting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month average consistently exceeds 1500, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of these periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and the federal funds rate is rising, it indicates a tightening monetary policy in response to a strong labor market and potential inflationary pressures. This can cool economic growth and negatively impact equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over any 5-year rolling window.
